description: Root Cause and Debug Hints priority rules for DMR.

_condition_operators_reference._summary: All conditions in a rule must be satisfied (implicit AND). Three operators work on string fields; boolean flags use direct equality.

_rule_actions.override: Default. Full replacement of priority order; evaluation stops at first match.
_rule_actions.adjust_last: Moves 'targets' IPs to the end of the current order. All matching adjust rules are applied (in declaration order) after override rules. Composable with other rules.
_rule_actions.adjust_first: Moves 'targets' IPs to the front of the current order. Same evaluation semantics as adjust_last.

_string_operators.{field}_in: Value is in the provided list.        e.g. "top_origreq_in": ["PortIn", "PortOut"]
_string_operators.{field}_contains: Substring present anywhere in value.  e.g. "core_mcas_contains": "bank"

_string_fields: top_origreq  – Top OrigReq value (e.g. PortIn, Read, RdCur)
top_opcode   – Top OpCode value  (e.g. RdCur, WrPush)
core_mcas    – Core top MC DECODE value
io_mcas      – IO top MC DECODE value (e.g. 'MCE when MCIP bit is set (MSCOD=2)')
mem_mcas     – MEM top MC DECODE value
_boolean_fields: other_present      – true when Other is non-empty

rules.name: CHA (CCF) error with MEM — MEM is likely root cause
rules.description: When both CCF and HA/HSF MCAs are present, the SCA→HAMVF dependency means HA timeout is typically the root cause. Promote mem above cha.

rules.name: IO and MEM both present
rules.description: TODO: Tune once DMR data characterizes whether HA or ULA failures are primary.

rules.name: Opcode PortIn/PortOut with CHA — demote CHA
rules.description: DMR CCF equivalent of the GNR PortIn/PortOut rule. In DMR, OrigReq is combined with Opcode (no MC_MISC3). Uses top_opcode instead.

rules.name: Writeback or Flush opcode with CHA and Core — Core is root cause
rules.description: Opcodes WbMtoI, WbEFtoI, CLFlush, CLWB, LLCWBInv etc. are Core-initiated operations writing data to the CHA/LLC. The CHA error is a downstream side effect. Promote Core above CHA.

rules.name: Lock or SplitLock opcode with CHA — Core is root cause
rules.description: Lock and SplitLock are atomic operations initiated by a Core. The CHA is a victim of the serialization. Demote CHA.

rules.name: Demand Read or RFO opcode with CHA — CHA is root cause
rules.description: CRd, DRd, RFO mean Core is waiting for CHA to fill a cache line. The Core 3-strike MCA is a downstream victim of CHA failing to respond. CHA is root cause.

rules.name: Core-only fail — pure Core hardware defect
rules.description: Core MCAs (IFU, DCU, DTLB) firing without any CHA or MEM hint indicate a pure Core hardware defect (pipeline, cache, TLB). Core is unambiguously root cause.

rules._TODO: Requires cha_mscod condition support in MCAAnalyzer
rules.name: CCF TA Overflow with Core — Core hammering CCF
IO MCIP bit set — deprioritize IO
rules.description: When IO MCAs (MC DECODE) shows 'MCE when MCIP bit is set (MSCOD=2)', the IO error is a secondary victim of another failure. Move IO to the last position so the actual root cause surfaces. This is an adjust_last rule — it composes on top of whichever override rule already fired, leaving the relative ordering of all other IPs unchanged.
